title: Resolving Route Parameter Middleware Issue in Express API

statement.markdown: Implement a simple Express API endpoint `/users/:id` that retrieves a user by their ID. The API endpoint should use a custom middleware function `authUser` to authenticate the user before retrieving their details. However, the current implementation has a logical bug that prevents it from working as expected. Your task is to fix this bug and ensure the API endpoint returns the user details correctly when a valid user ID is passed in the request.
statement.inputFormat: The API endpoint will receive a GET request with a user ID as a path parameter.
statement.outputFormat: The API endpoint should return the user details in JSON format.
statement.notes: Time complexity should be O(1) since we are dealing with a simple API endpoint. The solution should handle both valid and invalid user IDs correctly.

constraints.description: User ID passed in the request

examples.explanation: Valid user ID returned with user details
Invalid user ID returned with an error message

hints.text: Check the order of middleware functions and their functionality
Understand the difference between `express.Router()` and `app` instances
Verify user ID validation and error handling

editorial.title: Resolving Route Parameter Middleware Issue in Express API
editorial.intuition: The correct approach involves understanding the middleware function order and its impact on API endpoint functionality.
editorial.bruteForce: One possible solution is to use a separate middleware function to handle user ID validation and error handling.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution would be to use a single middleware function to handle both user ID validation and error handling.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution would be to use a single middleware function with a try-catch block to handle user ID validation and error handling.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: This solution ensures that the API endpoint returns the user details correctly when a valid user ID is passed in the request.
editorial.dryRun: Sample dry run with user ID 123: `/users/123` -> User details returned in JSON format
